description: Generates actionable remediation recommendations based on detected secrets and PII findings, including key rotation steps, notification procedures, and compliance remediation

remediation_items.action: Rotate AWS credentials and migrate to secrets manager
remediation_items.detailed_steps: 1. Log into AWS IAM Console immediately. 2. Deactivate the exposed access key. 3. Generate a new access key pair. 4. Store new credentials in AWS Secrets Manager or environment variables. 5. Update application configuration to read from secrets manager. 6. Add config/settings.py to .gitignore. 7. Use git filter-branch or BFG to remove the key from git history. 8. Audit CloudTrail logs for unauthorized usage of the exposed key.
remediation_items.compliance_notes: SOX compliance requires documented credential rotation procedures and audit trail. Record the incident in your security log.

remediation_items.action: Remove real SSN and replace with synthetic test data
remediation_items.detailed_steps: 1. Replace real SSN with a synthetic value (e.g., 000-00-0000 or use a faker library). 2. Scrub git history using BFG Repo-Cleaner. 3. Assess if the SSN belongs to a real individual. 4. If real, initiate breach notification procedure per CCPA/GDPR requirements. 5. Implement pre-commit hooks to detect SSN patterns.
remediation_items.compliance_notes: GDPR Article 33 requires breach notification within 72 hours. CCPA requires notification to affected individuals.
remediation_items.timeline: Immediate - within 2 hours, breach notification within 72 hours if real data

example_response.overall_recommendation: Critical findings require immediate action. Rotate all exposed credentials within 1 hour, remove all PII from codebase, and implement pre-commit hooks with secret scanning to prevent future exposure.
